claims.claim_text: In a winter wheat-ecofallow-corn-fallow rotation across Nebraska, surface mulch from the preceding winter wheat crop increased corn growth and yield; a mulch level of 3.4 to 5.1 t/ha increased corn yield by 14 to 15% over unmulched corn.
claims.evidence_text: Research was conducted to determine the effect of winter wheat straw mulch levels of 0, 1.7, 3.4, 5.1, and 6.8 t ha –1 and metolachlor rates of 0, 0.5, 1.0, and 1.5X-rates on corn growth and yield in a winter wheat-ecofallow-corn-fallow rotation at three locations across Nebraska.

context.yield_increase: 14 to 15% at 3.4-5.1 t/ha mulch over unmulched corn

mechanisms: increased soil moisture from wheat residue mulch after tasseling

provenance.source_title: Influence of Wheat (Triticum aestivum) Straw Mulch and Metolachlor on Corn (Zea mays) Growth and Yield | Weed Science | Cambridge Core
